commit.message:
Log game name for sus slash commands (#4536)

* Log game name for sus slash commands

Include the game name when reporting sus slash commands to the sus-slash-commands-log channel by adding a gameName parameter to reportToSusSlashCommandLog and updating the call site. Also remove the redundant two-argument sendMessageToGMChannel overload in GMService, consolidating to the (Game, String, boolean) signature (callers should pass false for no ping). This clarifies which game a private command came from and simplifies GM message handling.

* Update MessageListener.java

* Return early on invalid deletes; minor formatting

Fix a bug in DeletionListener by returning early when validateEvent(event) is false so invalid delete events are not scheduled for handling. Also simplify control flow by early-returning when GameManager.isValid(gameName) is false to reduce nesting; behavior for removing command-evidence messages and logging remains unchanged. Additionally, reflowed parameter and string concatenation formatting in SusSlashCommandService for readability.

* Update src/main/java/ti4/listeners/DeletionListener.java

commit.message:
fix: upgrade HaaLeo publish action v1→v2 in all 4 publish workflows (#275)

## Why?

All 4 publish workflows have been broken since HaaLeo v1.7.0
(`f4ece70f`) introduced a `generateFileStructureTree` crash (`TypeError:
Cannot read properties of undefined`). Nothing has published since Dec
2025.

PR #267 was opened for syntax-highlighting but auto-closed when the
branch briefly matched master SHA during a rebase. This PR covers all 4.

## What does this PR do?

Upgrades `HaaLeo/publish-vscode-extension` from `f4ece70f` (v1) to
`ca5561da` (v2) in all 4 publish workflows:
- `kusto-language-server-publish.yml`
- `kusto-color-themes-publish.yml`
- `kusto-extensions-pack-publish.yml`
- `kusto-syntax-highlighting-publish.yml`

Zero functional change — 4× the same 1-line SHA swap.

commit.message:
fix: reject batch creation when account balance is negative (#799)

## Summary
- Adds an upfront credit balance check to the batch creation endpoint
- Returns **402 Payment Required** when user's balance is below zero
- Zero balances are still permitted — only strictly negative balances
are rejected
- Also updates error enrichment to only classify 402s if credits are
less than zero.

Previously, batches from users with negative balances were accepted but
all requests would instantly fail during processing. This gives
immediate feedback instead of silently failing later.

## Test plan
- [x] `test_create_batch_rejected_with_negative_balance` — verifies 402
when balance < 0
- [x] `test_create_batch_allowed_with_zero_balance` — verifies zero
balance still succeeds
- [x] `just lint rust` passes

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Consolidate thing registrations into shared TemplatesPlugin

Move all ThingRegistry registrations (kinds 0-3) from creatures, items,
world_setup, and client overrides into a single TemplatesPlugin in
bins/shared/src/templates.rs. This eliminates the fragile pattern where
each module registered physics-only builders that the client had to
override with full visual+physics+gameplay builders.

Also consolidates parse_log_level and LogPlugin::from into shared config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move item events to own stream and fix headless server physics

Item-related network types (ItemActionEvent, ItemEvent, PendingItemEvents)
and systems (broadcast_item_event, held-item catch-up) moved from things
to items module with a new dedicated stream (tag 5, ItemsStreamMessage).
This removes cross-domain coupling where item wire types lived in things
due to the one-way crate dependency.

Also fix headless server missing floor colliders -- spawn_tile_meshes was
skipped entirely on headless, so all entities with gravity fell through
the world. Added spawn_tile_colliders for headless-only tile physics.

commit.message:
feat(ui): add confirmation dialog for destructive configuration parameters (#4572)

Z-Wave config parameters can be marked `destructive` (e.g., factory
reset commands) — setting them remotely may leave the device needing
physical intervention to recover. The UI should gate writes on these
parameters behind an explicit user confirmation.

## Changes

- **`api/lib/ZwaveClient.ts`**
  - Added `destructive?: boolean` to `ZUIValueId` type
- Propagate `ConfigurationMetadata.destructive` into the value model in
`_updateValueMetadata`

- **`src/components/nodes-table/ExpandedNode.vue`**
- In `updateValue()`, check `v.destructive` and show an `alert`-level
confirm dialog before proceeding with the `writeValue` API call;
cancelling aborts the write cleanly

commit.message:
Fix Docker CI: move TAG env to step-level and chain workflow_call for releases (#11)

* Initial plan

* fix: Docker CI trigger on release and TypeScript CI trigger on PR

- Add workflow_call trigger to docker.yaml so release.yml can call it
  directly, bypassing the GITHUB_TOKEN limitation that prevents
  release events from triggering other workflows
- Update release.yml to call docker.yaml after release-please creates
  a release, passing the tag name and inheriting secrets
- Add push trigger on main branch to typescript.yaml so TypeScript
  checks run on both PRs and pushes to main

Co-authored-by: orochibraru <52313840+orochibraru@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add 'latest' fallback to TAG expression for pull_request events

Co-authored-by: orochibraru <52313840+orochibraru@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move TAG env to step-level to prevent empty value on PR builds

The expression `${{ inputs.tag || github.event.release.tag_name }}`
evaluates to empty string on pull_request events. This empty string
overrides docker-bake.hcl's default TAG of "latest" and fails the
HCL validation `TAG != ""`.

Move TAG from job-level env to step-level env on the "Build and push"
step only, which only runs on non-PR events where the tag is available.
On PR builds, docker-bake.hcl's own default "latest" is used naturally.

Co-authored-by: orochibraru <52313840+orochibraru@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): show "unknown" for null/undefined values with units; fix battery level null display (#4569) When a non-compliant device responds to a battery level request with only a "battery low" notification, `minBatteryLevel` ends up as `null`. Two bugs compound: JS coerces `null` to `0` in numeric comparisons (`null <= 10 === true`), causing the battery column to show `"null%"` with a red error icon; and `ValueId.vue`'s readonly display concatenates the unit unconditionally, producing `"null %"` or `"undefined %"`. ## Changes - **`ValueId.vue`** — Guard the readonly display expression: if `parsedValue == null`, render `"unknown"` instead of `parsedValue + ' '`. ```diff - parsedValue + (modelValue.unit ? ' ' + modelValue.unit : '') + parsedValue == null + ? 'unknown' + : parsedValue + (modelValue.unit ? ' ' + modelValue.unit : '') ``` - **`nodes-table.js` / `SmartView.vue`** — In `powerRichValue`, add an explicit `level == null` guard before the numeric `if`/`else if` chain. When null/undefined: show the battery-unknown icon, set `description = 'Battery level: unknown'`, and leave `label` empty (no text in the battery column). Also removed the now-unreachable `else` branch that previously followed `level > 90`.
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > Battery level reported as above 100% (e.g. 247%, 254%) for low battery condition > ### Checklist > > - [x] I am not using Home Assistant. Or: a developer has told me to come here. > - [x] I have checked the troubleshooting section and my problem is not described there. > - [x] I have read the changelog and my problem is not mentioned there. > > ### Deploy method > > Snap > > ### Z-Wave JS UI version > > 11.4.1 > > ### ZwaveJS version > > 15.15.3 > > ### Describe the bug > > For battery-operated deviced (in my case these are Danfoss LC-13 thermostats) when battery is low, Z-Wave JS is reporting the battery levels as percentages above 240%, e.g. node 8 is 254%, node 9 is 247%. This happens both in the UI as well as in the MQTT messages being sent. Sample screenshot below and logs attached. > > [battery-zwave-issue-20251102.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23289592/battery-zwave-issue-20251102.zip) > > > > ### To Reproduce > > have battery with low voltage in thermostat > > ### Expected behavior > > show 0% instead of abnormally %% levels > > ### Additional context > > when battery isn't low, it's showing correct percentage (e.g. 40%, 100%, etc) > > For values that have a unit, prevent the UI from showing `null ` and `undefined ` when the actual value is unknown. Instead show "unknown". > > Treat the battery level on the nodes table as a special case and show no text instead. > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > @robertsLando > Please make a [driver log](https://zwave-js.github.io/zwave-js-ui/#/troubleshooting/generating-logs?id=driver-logs), loglevel `debug` and attach it here as a file (drag & drop into the text field). > > cc @AlCalzone > @AlCalzone > I can imagine what's going on there. The device probably has a simple linear function to estimate the battery level from voltage and happens to have worked below the level that's supposed to be 0%, so it ends up sending something like -2% (254) or so. > > The linked PR adds validation for this specific case to make sure we ignore those reports altogether. > @AlCalzone > None of the problematic nodes report any battery level but "battery low" in that log. Any chance you have older ones? > FWIW, this is just for curiosity - the issue should no longer happen after the next update. You'll have to change batteries though for this to take effect, because it needs a valid battery report to clear up. > @AlCalzone > Please make a [driver log](https://zwave-js.github.io/zwave-js-ui/#/troubleshooting/generating-logs?id=driver-logs), loglevel `debug` and attach it here as a file (drag & drop into the text field). > > Include a re-interview and such a battery report in the log. > @AlCalzone > I missed this. During the interview, when asked for the battery level, the device just responded with the notification that the battery level is low. It **should** have returned the actual level, but didn't. > > Given the non-compliant behavior, I think this is merely a UI bug. `null %` and `undefined %` shouldn't be shown, rather "unknown". > >

commit.message:
Fix: Call EnsureTablesExistAsync at Web startup (missing Assets table)

The Web app never called EnsureTablesExistAsync() - only the MAUI app did.
This caused 'no such table: Assets' errors because the Assets, Portfolios,
and related tables are created via raw SQL in that method, not via
EF Core's OnModelCreating.

Added Phase 0.5 in Web startup to ensure all DB tables exist before
any service initialization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Restore --unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure flag for Blazor WASM Lighthouse scenario (#2157)

* Restore --unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure flag for Blazor WASM Lighthouse scenario

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Also accept cached (fromCache) responses in WASM caching check

The WASM caching enforcement check previously only accepted HTTP 304
(Not Modified) as proof that a .wasm resource was cached. However,
browsers may serve cached resources with HTTP 200 from disk/memory
cache without making a network round-trip at all. Puppeteer exposes
this via response.fromCache(). Both cases indicate the resource was
properly cached after the first page load, so both should be accepted.

Without this fix, the 'Second page load' step fails with:
  Unexpected uncached wasm resource '...System.Runtime.InteropServices.JavaScript.*.wasm'
even though the resource is served from the browser cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
